factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, which have contributed positively to sales growth.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, which is harmful to overall performance.

evidence.text: Sales from existing businesses in the segment’s Advanced Instrumentation & Solutions businesses declined at a mid-single digit rate during both the three and six months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the comparable periods of 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, negatively impacting performance.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining in certain product lines, driven by lower demand in key markets.

evidence.text: Field solutions sales declined at a low-single digit rate during the three months ended July 1, 2016 and at a mid-single digit rate during the six months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the comparable periods of 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are declining in certain product lines due to lower demand.

factors.summary: End markets such as Europe and China are showing growth, while North America and Latin America are experiencing declines.

evidence.text: Sales from existing businesses in the segment’s Advanced Instrumentation & Solutions businesses increased year-over-year in China and Western Europe which were more than offset by sales declines in the United States and Latin America during both the three and six month periods ended July 1, 2016.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Growth in some regions is offset by declines in others, resulting in mixed momentum.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, as evidenced by declines in existing businesses. The deceleration is noted in both the three-month and six-month periods compared to the previous year.

evidence.text: Sales from existing businesses decreased 0.5% for the three months ended July 1, 2016 compared to the comparable period of 2015, indicating a deceleration in organic growth.

Sales from existing businesses in the segment’s Advanced Instrumentation & Solutions businesses declined at a mid-single digit rate during both the three and six months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the comparable periods of 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Deceleration in organic revenue growth is evident from the declines in existing businesses.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with some improvement in certain end markets but an overall decline in others. Despite sequential improvement in demand during the three months ended July 1, 2016, there was an aggregate year-over-year decline in sales from existing businesses.

evidence.text: Demand for the Company’s products, software and services declined during the three months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the comparable period of 2015 resulting in aggregate year-over-year decline in sales from existing businesses of 0.5%
The business experienced sequential improvement in demand during the three months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the three months ended April 1, 2016.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends show mixed results with some improvement but an overall decline year-over-year.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are presenting a mixed outlook, acting as a headwind in some regions while showing growth in others. The global economy and the markets Fortive serves are facing challenges, contributing to a decline in demand for the Company’s products and services. Sales from existing businesses in the segment’s Advanced Instrumentation & Solutions businesses increased year-over-year in China and Western Europe, which were more than offset by sales declines in the United States and Latin America during both the three and six month periods ended July 1, 2016.

evidence.text: The global economy and the markets Fortive serves are facing challenges, contributing to a decline in demand for the Company’s products and services.

Sales from existing businesses in the segment’s Advanced Instrumentation & Solutions businesses increased year-over-year in China and Western Europe which were more than offset by sales declines in the United States and Latin America during both the three and six month periods ended July 1, 2016.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Economic conditions are challenging with declines in some regions offsetting growth in others.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a compression in both gross and operating margins, which is attributed to factors such as lower sales volumes, currency effects, and restructuring costs. While the critical accounting estimates remain stable, this does not offset the negative margin trend observed in the operational results.

evidence.text: Gross and operating margins are compressing.
Operating profit margins were 20.7% for the three months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to 21.5% in the comparable period of 2015, indicating a compression in margins.
Operating profit margins decreased 230 basis points during the three months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the comparable period of 2015.

There were no material changes during the six month period ended July 1, 2016 to the items the Company disclosed as its critical accounting policies and estimates in 'Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations' in the Company’s Information Statement.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is facing margin compression due to lower sales and other factors.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving due to both higher cash flows from operating activities and increased cash provided by financing activities.

evidence.text: Cash provided by financing activities increased $404 million during the six months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the comparable period in 2015, due to the Company’s incurrence of approximately $3.4 billion of indebtedness as described above and more fully in Note 4 of the Combined Condensed Financial Statements offset by payments and transfers to Parent.
Cash flows from operations increased $103 million, or approximately 25%, during the six months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the comparable period of 2015.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow is improving due to higher cash flows from operations and financing.

factors.summary: The company is actively managing its debt structure, showing signs of deleveraging through financing activities and refinancing at lower rates. However, the availability of additional indebtedness indicates potential future borrowing capacity.

evidence.text: The carrying value of total debt outstanding as of July 1, 2016 was approximately $3.4 billion. The Company had the ability to incur approximately an additional $1.1 billion of indebtedness based on the amounts available under the Company’s $1.5 billion Revolving Credit Facility which was not being used to backstop outstanding commercial paper balances as of July 1, 2016.

The cash balance presented on the Combined Condensed Balance Sheet as of July 1, 2016 of $487 million represents amounts clearly associated with Fortive related to the contribution of the Fortive businesses to the Company on July 1, 2016, as described above.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company is managing debt but has the capacity to borrow more, indicating a cautious approach.

factors.summary: The company has a mixed outlook on capital expenditures. While it may increase capex, signaling confidence in future growth, it has set a cautious target of $110 million to $130 million for 2016, indicating a measured approach to expansion.

evidence.text: The Company will have cash requirements to support working capital needs, capital expenditures and acquisitions, pay interest and service debt, pay taxes and any related interest or penalties, fund its restructuring activities and pension plans as required, and support other business needs or objectives. With respect to the Company’s other cash requirements, the Company generally intends to use available cash and internally generated funds to meet these cash requirements, but in the event that additional liquidity is required, particularly in connection with acquisitions, the Company may also borrow under its commercial paper programs or credit facilities, enter into new credit facilities and either borrow directly thereunder or use such credit facilities to backstop additional borrowing capacity under its commercial paper programs and/or access the capital markets.
For the full year 2016, the Company expects capital spending to be between $110 million and $130 million, though actual expenditures will ultimately depend on business conditions.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company has a cautious target for capex, indicating a mixed outlook.
